inline_review:
commit 7564ba79e805ceeebd96709ac66bb18bd769b527
Author: David Rientjes <rientjes@google.com>

mm, page_alloc: reintroduce page allocation stall warning

This commit reintroduces a warning when a single page allocation takes
longer than 10 seconds. The warning is rate-limited to trigger at most
once every 10 seconds globally across the system to prevent spamming the
kernel log during high memory pressure.

> --- a/mm/page_alloc.c
> +++ b/mm/page_alloc.c
> @@ -282,6 +282,14 @@ EXPORT_SYMBOL(nr_node_ids);
>  EXPORT_SYMBOL(nr_online_nodes);
>  #endif
>  
> +/*
> + * When page allocations stall for longer than a threshold,
> + * ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS, leave a warning in the kernel log.  Only one warning
> + * will be printed during this duration for the entire system.
> + */
> +#define ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS (10 * 1000UL)
> +static unsigned long alloc_stall_warn_jiffies;

Does this variable need explicit initialization to INITIAL_JIFFIES?

Because it is a static variable, it is implicitly zero-initialized. The
system jiffies counter is initialized to INITIAL_JIFFIES (typically
-300 * HZ).

This means 0 represents a time 300 seconds in the future relative to boot,
so the rate-limiting check below will evaluate to true and silently suppress
warnings for the first ~5 minutes of uptime.

> +
>  static bool page_contains_unaccepted(struct page *page, unsigned int order);
>  static bool cond_accept_memory(struct zone *zone, unsigned int order,

[ ... ]

> @@ -4693,6 +4701,40 @@ check_retry_cpuset(int cpuset_mems_cookie, struct alloc_context *ac)
>  	return false;
>  }
>  
> +static void check_alloc_stall_warn(gfp_t gfp_mask, nodemask_t *nodemask,
> +				unsigned int order, unsigned long alloc_start_time)
> +{
> +	static DEFINE_SPINLOCK(alloc_stall_lock);
> +	unsigned long stall_msecs = jiffies_to_msecs(jiffies - alloc_start_time);
> +
> +	if (likely(stall_msecs < ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS))
> +		return;
> +	if (time_before(jiffies, READ_ONCE(alloc_stall_warn_jiffies)))
> +		return;

Can this rate-limiting check fail on 32-bit systems after long periods
without any stalls?

If no stall occurs for LONG_MAX jiffies (about 24.8 days on 32-bit systems
with HZ=1000), jiffies - alloc_stall_warn_jiffies will exceed LONG_MAX.
Due to signed integer overflow in the time_before() macro, the difference
becomes negative.

This would cause time_before() to incorrectly evaluate to true, silently
suppressing all stall warnings for the subsequent ~24.8 days until the
difference wraps back to a positive value.
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Implicit zero-initialization of `alloc_stall_warn_jiffies` suppresses warnings for the first 5 minutes of system uptime.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"In the Linux kernel, the `jiffies` counter is initialized to `INITIAL_JIFFIES` (typically `-300 * HZ`) to detect timer wrap-around bugs early. Because the static variable `alloc_stall_warn_jiffies` is implicitly initialized to 0, the lockless check `time_before(jiffies, READ_ONCE(alloc_stall_warn_jiffies))` evaluates to `time_before(INITIAL_JIFFIES, 0)`. Since 0 represents a time 300 seconds in the future relative to `INITIAL_JIFFIES`, this check evaluates to true, causing the function to immediately return. This silently suppresses all page allocation stall warnings during early boot and the first ~5 minutes of uptime. This is a non-critical logic error resulting in missed diagnostics."},{"problem":"Rate-limiting logic fails on 32-bit systems when no stalls occur for `LONG_MAX` jiffies, incorrectly suppressing warnings for ~24.8 days.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"The code tracks the next allowed warning time in the absolute timestamp `alloc_stall_warn_jiffies`. If no stall occurs for `LONG_MAX` jiffies (~24.8 days on 32-bit systems with HZ=1000), `jiffies - alloc_stall_warn_jiffies` will exceed `LONG_MAX`. Due to signed integer overflow in the `time_before()` macro, this difference becomes negative, making `time_before(jiffies, alloc_stall_warn_jiffies)` incorrectly evaluate to true. This will silently suppress all stall warnings for the subsequent ~24.8 days until the difference wraps back to a positive value. This is a known pitfall with absolute timestamp tracking that intermittently disables the warning mechanism on 32-bit architectures."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 7564ba79e805ceeebd96709ac66bb18bd769b527

> + */
> +#define ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS (10 * 1000UL)
> +static unsigned long alloc_stall_warn_jiffies;

Because it is a static variable, it is implicitly zero-initialized. The

-300 * HZ).

This means 0 represents a time 300 seconds in the future relative to boot,
so the rate-limiting check below will evaluate to true and silently suppress
warnings for the first ~5 minutes of uptime.

> +
>  static bool page_contains_unaccepted(struct page *page, unsigned int order);
>  static bool cond_accept_memory(struct zone *zone, unsigned int order,

[ ... ]

>  	return false;
>  }
>  
> +static void check_alloc_stall_warn(gfp_t gfp_mask, nodemask_t *nodemask,
> +				unsigned int order, unsigned long alloc_start_time)
> +{
> +	static DEFINE_SPINLOCK(alloc_stall_lock);
> +	unsigned long stall_msecs = jiffies_to_msecs(jiffies - alloc_start_time);
> +
> +	if (likely(stall_msecs < ALLOC_STALL_WARN_MSECS))
> +		return;
> +	if (time_before(jiffies, READ_ONCE(alloc_stall_warn_jiffies)))
> +		return;

Can this rate-limiting check fail on 32-bit systems after long periods
without any stalls?

If no stall occurs for LONG_MAX jiffies (about 24.8 days on 32-bit systems
with HZ=1000), jiffies - alloc_stall_warn_jiffies will exceed LONG_MAX.

This would cause time_before() to incorrectly evaluate to true, silently
suppressing all stall warnings for the subsequent ~24.8 days until the
